title: mibora minima subsp. littorea
intro: Mibora minima subsp. littorea, also known as sea oat grass or littoral brome, is a subspecies of the Mibora minima plant, a type of grass native to the coastal regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Mibora minima subsp. littorea include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against infection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
